title: Restart a Selected Robot From the Robot Selection Screen
summary: Use the Robot Selection screen to restart a specific robot by entering the robot ID or selecting the robot, clicking Restart, confirming the robot is at the restart point or on the QR code cell as stated by the source, and verifying that after starting the positioned robot is displayed on the station map.
when_to_use: Use when a specific robot needs to be restarted from the Robot Selection screen and the operator needs to verify the robot is displayed on the station map after startup.

responsibilities: Identify the robot by entering the robot ID or selecting the robot
Verify the position requirement stated by the source
Confirm the robot appears on the station map after starting

safety_notes: Stop and escalate if the robot is not at the restart point or on the QR code cell as required by the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Robot Selection screen
Robot ID or ability to select the robot on screen

access_required: Access to the Robot Selection screen
system_preconditions: The target robot can be identified by robot ID or selected on the Robot Selection screen
The robot is at the restart point or on the QR code cell as stated on the source slide

visual_references.description: Training slide frame showing the Robot Selection restart workflow, including robot ID entry or robot selection, Restart action, restart position requirement, and post-start map verification.

steps.title: Open the Robot Selection restart screen
steps.purpose: Access the HMI area used to restart a selected robot.
steps.instruction: Open or use the Robot Selection screen with the Restart Robot function.
steps.expected_result: The Robot Selection screen and Restart Robot area are available for use.
steps.healthy_condition: The Robot Selection screen is visible and the Restart Robot function is present.
steps.failure_condition: The Robot Selection screen or Restart Robot function is not available.

actions.label: Open Robot Selection
actions.exact_action: Open or use the "Robot Selection" screen with the "Restart Robot" function.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot Selection screen and Restart Robot area.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Robot Selection screen or Restart Robot function cannot be accessed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide shows 'Robot Selection' and 'Restart Robot' with text indicating entry of robot ID or selection of the robot, followed by clicking Restart.

steps.title: Identify the robot to restart
steps.purpose: Select the correct robot for the restart action.
steps.instruction: Enter the corresponding robot ID or select the robot from the screen.
steps.expected_result: The intended robot is selected for restart.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct robot ID is entered or the correct robot is selected on the screen.
steps.failure_condition: The robot cannot be identified or the wrong robot may be selected.

actions.label: Select robot
actions.exact_action: Enter the corresponding robot ID or select the robot from the screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot ID entry or robot selection area on the Robot Selection screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The correct robot cannot be identified from the screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide shows 'Robot Selection' and 'Restart Robot' with text indicating entry of robot ID or selection of the robot, followed by clicking Restart.

steps.title: Initiate the restart
steps.purpose: Start the restart process for the selected robot.
steps.expected_result: The restart command is issued for the selected robot.
steps.healthy_condition: The Restart action is accepted for the selected robot.
steps.failure_condition: The Restart action cannot be initiated or does not apply to the selected robot.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Restart control on the Robot Selection screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Restart action is unavailable for the selected robot.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide shows 'Robot Selection' and 'Restart Robot' with text indicating entry of robot ID or selection of the robot, followed by clicking Restart.

steps.title: Confirm the restart position requirement
steps.purpose: Verify the source-stated condition associated with a successful restart.
steps.instruction: Verify the robot is at the restart point or on the QR code cell as stated on the slide.
steps.expected_result: The robot meets the position requirement stated by the source for restart.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot is at the restart point or on the QR code cell.
steps.failure_condition: The robot is not at the restart point and not on the QR code cell.

actions.label: Verify restart position condition
actions.exact_action: Verify the robot is at the restart point or on the QR code cell as stated on the slide.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Slide text stating the robot will restart when it is at the restart point or on the QR code cell.

stop_or_escalate_if: The robot is not at the restart point or on the QR code cell as required by the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide text states: 'The robot will restart (it is at the restart point or on the QR code cell).'

steps.title: Verify the robot appears on the station map after starting
steps.purpose: Confirm the restart outcome shown by the source.
steps.instruction: After starting, check that the positioned robot is displayed on the station map.
steps.expected_result: The positioned robot is displayed on the station map after starting.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot appears on the station map after starting.
steps.failure_condition: The robot does not appear on the station map after starting.

actions.label: Check station map
actions.exact_action: After starting, check that the positioned robot is displayed on the station map.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Map display indication that the positioned robot is displayed after starting.

stop_or_escalate_if: The robot does not appear on the station map after starting.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide text states that after starting, the positioned robot will be displayed on the station map.

success_criteria: The selected robot is restarted from the Robot Selection screen.
The robot meets the source-stated position requirement of being at the restart point or on the QR code cell.
After starting, the positioned robot is displayed on the station map.
healthy_conditions: The Robot Selection screen and Restart Robot function are available.
The correct robot can be identified by robot ID or selection on screen.
The robot is at the restart point or on the QR code cell.
The positioned robot is displayed on the station map after starting.
failure_conditions: The Robot Selection screen or Restart Robot function is not available.
The correct robot cannot be identified or selected.
The robot is not at the restart point or on the QR code cell.
The robot does not appear on the station map after starting.
escalation_guidance: Stop and escalate if the robot is not at the restart point or on the QR code cell as required by the source.
Escalate if the robot does not appear on the station map after starting.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot Selection restart workflow, restart position requirement, and post-start station map verification.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Slide shows 'Robot Selection' and 'Restart Robot' with text indicating entry of robot ID or selection of the robot, followed by clicking Restart.
Slide text states: 'The robot will restart (it is at the restart point or on the QR code cell).'
Slide text states that after starting, the positioned robot will be displayed on the station map.

missing_details: The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not specify whether production stop is required.
The source does not specify whether lockout/tagout is required.

The source does not provide detailed troubleshooting steps beyond escalation when the position requirement or map display verification fails.
